data.title: Executives Express Growing Concerns Over AI Disruption in Earnings Calls
data.description: Executives are expressing heightened concerns about the potential disruption caused by artificial intelligence, as evidenced by a significant increase in mentions during recent earnings calls. Bloomberg posted on X that the frequency of AI-related discussions has nearly doubled in the latest quarter, reflecting the growing apprehension among business leaders. This surge in attention underscores the impact AI is expected to have on various industries, prompting companies to reassess their strategies and operations. As AI technology continues to evolve, executives are increasingly focused on understanding its implications and preparing for potential challenges. The rise in AI mentions highlights the urgency with which businesses are addressing this transformative technology.

data.title: UNESCO-Listed Argungu Fishing Festival Returns After Six Years
data.description: The Argungu Fishing Festival, recognized by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage, has resumed in Kebbi State, Nigeria, after a six-year hiatus. According to Ming Pao, the event drew significant attention when local fisherman Abubakar Usman caught a massive fish weighing 59 kilograms, becoming the highlight of the festival.

data.title: China Announces Visa-Free Entry for Canadian and British Citizens Starting 2026
data.description: China has decided to implement a visa-free policy for Canadian and British citizens holding ordinary passports, effective from February 17, 2026. According to Jin10, this initiative aims to facilitate easier travel and exchanges between China and these countries. The policy allows Canadian and British passport holders to enter China for business, tourism, family visits, and transit without a visa for stays of up to 30 days. This visa exemption will be in effect until December 31, 2026.

data.title: Ether holds as Buterin backs hedging-first markets
data.description:
Vitalik Buterin urges a shift from speculation to prediction markets hedging, interest-bearing collateral, CFTC regulation and fiat-free settlement, use cases.
source: https://coincu.com/news/ether-holds-as-buterin-backs-hedging-first-markets/

data.title: Lightning Labs Launches Open-Source Toolkit for AI Bitcoin Payments
data.description:
Lightning Labs has introduced an open-source toolkit enabling AI agents to autonomously send and receive Bitcoin payments via the Lightning Network. According to PANews, this development eliminates the need for human approval, accounts, login credentials, or API keys.

The toolkit utilizes the L402 protocol, which is based on the HTTP 402 'Payment Required' standard. Websites can issue Lightning Network invoices to AI agents, who, upon payment, receive digital payment credentials as access keys to unlock services or data. Additionally, the toolkit includes the 'lnget' tool, which automatically handles requests for paid content access in the background.

The toolkit also supports AI in running Lightning nodes, managing permissions, hosting paid services, and completing transactions between agents. It features built-in spending limits and remote signing protection to enhance security.

data.title: Robinhood launches Arbitrum-based blockchain public testnet
data.description:
Robinhood launched a public testnet for its Arbitrum-based blockchain, advancing its push into tokenized assets and onchain financial infrastructure.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/robinhood-launches-arbitrum-based-blockchain-public-testnet/

data.title: Winter Olympics in Milan Faces Unexpected Shortage of Free Condoms
data.description: The Winter Olympics in Milan-Cortina, coinciding with Valentine's Day, has seen an unexpected shortage of free condoms in the athletes' village. According to Ming Pao, the distribution of complimentary condoms, a tradition at the Games, became a topic of discussion as supplies ran out earlier than anticipated, catching organizers off guard.

data.title: Zand and Ripple have entered into a strategic partnership to promote the development of the UAE's stablecoin and digital economy.
data.description: Zand, the digital bank of the UAE, announced a strategic partnership with Ripple to drive the region's digital economy through stablecoins and blockchain solutions. The two companies will collaborate on innovative initiatives surrounding Zand's AEDZ stablecoin and Ripple's RLUSD stablecoin. Under the agreement, they will explore supporting RLUSD within Zand's regulated digital asset custody service and investigate direct liquidity solutions between AEDZ and RLUSD. Furthermore, they plan to issue AEDZ on the XRP Ledger (XRPL). Zand CEO Michael Chan stated that leveraging stablecoins and tokenization technology unlocks new use cases for bringing traditional finance to the blockchain. AEDZ is the UAE's first regulated, multi-chain AED-collateralized stablecoin, backed 1:1 by the AED reserve. This collaboration aims to help the UAE achieve its goal of doubling its digital economy contribution by 2032. (The Fintech Times)

data.title: Japanese Financial Giants Initiate Stablecoin Trial for Stock and Fund Transactions
data.description: Five major Japanese financial institutions, including Mizuho Financial Group and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group, announced last Friday, the 13th, the launch of a stablecoin trial. According to Ming Pao, this initiative, starting this month, aims to use stablecoins for trading stocks and funds. The trial will assess the mechanisms required for this service and address related issues. Other participants in the trial include Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Nomura Securities, and Daiwa Securities.

data.title: UAE Digital Bank Zand Expands Collaboration with Ripple
data.description: UAE digital bank Zand has broadened its partnership with Ripple, according to ChainCatcher. The collaboration includes supporting RLUSD within Zand's regulated digital asset custody services. Additionally, Zand is researching direct liquidity solutions between the regulated Dirham stablecoin AEDZ and RLUSD, and exploring the issuance of AEDZ on the XRPL platform.

data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Brother Machi added 225 ETH to his position, bringing his total long position in ETH futures contracts to 2,925 ETH, worth approximately $6 million. He also opened a small number of VVV long positions, currently worth $22,000.

data.title: Insurance Industry Faces Growing Losses from Civil Unrest
data.description:
Civil unrest has emerged as a significant risk for the insurance industry, a category that was almost nonexistent over a decade ago. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing impact of civil disturbances on insurance losses. The industry is grappling with the financial implications of these events, which have become more frequent and severe in recent years.

Insurance companies are now facing substantial claims related to property damage and business interruptions caused by protests and riots. This trend has prompted insurers to reassess their risk models and coverage strategies to better manage potential losses.

The rise in civil unrest incidents has been attributed to various socio-political factors, including economic disparities and political tensions. As these events continue to unfold globally, the insurance sector is under pressure to adapt and find effective ways to mitigate the financial risks associated with civil disturbances.

Industry experts are urging insurers to develop more comprehensive policies that address the unique challenges posed by civil unrest. This includes enhancing risk assessment techniques and exploring innovative solutions to provide adequate coverage for affected businesses and individuals.

The growing prevalence of civil unrest underscores the need for the insurance industry to evolve and respond to changing risk landscapes. As insurers navigate this complex environment, they must balance the demands of policyholders with the realities of an increasingly volatile world.

data.title: Solana Spot ETFs Attract $13.17 Million in Net Inflows
data.description: Solana (SOL) spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have garnered significant investor interest, with a net inflow of $13.17 million recorded from February 9 to 13. According to NS3.AI, Bitwise's BSOL led the inflows, contributing $12.72 million, while Fidelity's FSOL added $0.73 million. Despite a minor net outflow from VanEck's VSOL, the total assets of SOL ETFs have increased to $721 million, accounting for 1.5% of Solana's market capitalization.

data.title: Tom Lee Predicts Recovery in Crypto Market by 2026
data.description:
On February 15, Tom Lee shared insights during an interview with Rug Radio founder Farokh Sarmad. According to BlockBeats, Lee described the current crypto market as experiencing a 'mini winter,' suggesting it is not a prolonged bear market but will require time to stabilize. He advised investors to start buying in batches at low points rather than waiting for a perfect bottom, noting that the market's challenges could persist for several months to half a year, with a significant turnaround expected by 2026.

Tom Lee expressed no regrets about purchasing ETH early, stating that ETH is near its bottom, potentially dipping briefly below $1800 or $1890. He highlighted that historically, there have been eight instances of V-shaped recoveries. Lee views ETH as a long-term investment, underpinning stablecoins, AI, and the creator economy over the next 15 years.

Lee also reiterated his prediction that Bitcoin could rise to $200,000-$250,000 by 2026, with Ethereum reaching $12,000-$22,000 based on historical ratios.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia Open to UN-Led Temporary Management in Ukraine
data.description: Russia's Deputy Foreign Minister, Galuzin, has expressed willingness to discuss the possibility of introducing a UN-led temporary external management mechanism in Ukraine with the United States, Europe, and other countries. According to Jin10, Galuzin stated that this approach could enable Ukraine to hold democratic elections and establish a government capable of signing peace treaties and other legitimate international cooperation agreements. He noted that similar precedents exist in UN peacekeeping operations. In March 2025, Russian President Vladimir Putin mentioned this as a feasible plan. However, Galuzin also indicated that the possibility of such an approach has not been publicly discussed recently.
